author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm CXLVII

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is said to us, “Praise the Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is said to all nations, not to us alone.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And these words, sounded forth through separate places by the Readers, each Church heareth separately; but the one same Voice of God proclaimeth unto all, that we praise Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And as though we asked wherefore we ought to praise the Lord, behold what reason he hath brought forward: “Praise the Lord,” he saith, “for a Psalm is good.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is this all the reward of them that praise?…The “Psalm” is praise of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This then he saith, “Praise the Lord, for it is good to praise the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us not thus pass over the praise of the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is spoken, and hath passed: it is done, and we are silent: we have praised, and then rested; we have sung, and then rested.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We go forth to some business which awaits us, and when other employments have found us, shall the praise of God cease in us?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not so: thy tongue praiseth but for a while, let thy life ever praise.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus then “a Psalm is good.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For a “Psalm” is a song, not any kind of song, but a song to a psaltery.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A psaltery is a kind of instrument of music, like the lyre and the harp, and such kinds of instruments, which were invented for music.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He therefore who singeth Psalms, not only singeth with his voice, but with a certain instrument besides, which is called a psaltery, he accompanieth his voice with his hands.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wilt thou then sing a Psalm?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let not thy voice alone sound the praises of God; but let thy works also be in harmony with thy voice.…To please then the ear, sing with thy voice; but with thy heart be not silent, with thy life be not still.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou devisest no fraud in thy heart: thou singest a Psalm to God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When thou eatest and drinkest, sing a Psalm: not by intermingling sweet sounds suited to the ear, but by eating and drinking moderately, frugally, temperately: for thus saith the Apostle, “whether ye eat or drink, or whatever ye do, do all to the glory of God.” …If by immoderate voracity thou exceedest the due bounds of nature, and gluttest thyself in excess of wine, however great praises of God thy tongue sound, yet thy life blasphemeth Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: After food and drink thou liest down to sleep: in thy bed neither commit any pollution, nor go beyond the license given by the law of God: let thy marriage bed be kept chaste with thy wife: and if thou desire to beget children, yet let there not be unbridled sensuality of lust: in thy bed give honour to thy wife, for ye are both members of Christ, both made by Him, both renewed by His Blood: so doing thou praisest God, nor will thy praise be altogether silent.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What, when sleep has come over thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let not an evil conscience rouse thee from rest: so doth the innocence of thy sleep praise God.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let praises be pleasant to our God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If He be praised by our good lives.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear that then praise will be pleasant to Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In another place it is said, “Praise is not seemly in the mouth of a sinner.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If then in the mouth of a sinner praise is not seemly, neither is it pleasant, for that only is pleasant which is seemly.…For praise may be pleasant to a man, when he heareth one praising with neat and clever sentiments, and with a sweet voice; but “let praise be pleasant to our God,” whose ears are open not to the mouth, but to the heart; not to the tongue, but to the life of him that praiseth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who is “our God,” that praise should be pleasant to Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He maketh Himself sweet to us, He commendeth Himself to us; thanks to His condescension.…“But God commendeth His love to us”…“in that, while we were yet sinners, Christ died for us.”…Let us see whether it be the commendation which the Apostle speaketh of, that Christ died for the sinners and ungodly: “the Lord who buildeth up Jerusalem, and gathereth the dispersions of Israel” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the people of Jerusalem are the people of Israel.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is Jerusalem “eternal in the heavens,” whereof the Angels are citizens also.…All the citizens then of that city, through “seeing God,” rejoice in that great and wide and heavenly city; they gaze upon God Himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But we are wanderers from that city, driven out by sin, that we should not remain there; weighed down by mortality, that we should not return thither.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God looked back on our wandering, and He who “buildeth up Jerusalem,” restored the part that had fallen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How restored He the part that had fallen?…He sent then to our captive estate His Son as a Redeemer.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Take with Thee, said He, a bag, bear therein the price of the captives.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He put on Him our mortal flesh, and therein was the Blood, by the shedding of which we were to be redeemed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With that Blood He “gathered the dispersions of Israel.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And if He gathered them that before were dispersed, how must we strive that they be gathered who now are dispersed?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If the dispersed have been gathered, that in the Hand of the Builder they might be fashioned into the building, how should they be gathered who through disquiet have fallen from the Hand of the Builder?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold whom we praise; behold to whom we owe praise all our life long.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How doth He gather?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What doeth He in order to gather?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who healeth the bruised in heart” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold the way in which the dispersions of Israel are gathered, by the healing of the bruised in heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They who are not of a bruised heart, are not healed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is to bruise the heart?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let it be known, brethren, let it be done, that ye may be able to be healed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is told in many other places of Scripture;…“the sacrifice of God is a troubled spirit, a bruised and contrite heart God will not despise.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He healeth then the bruised in heart, for He draweth nigh unto them to heal them; as is said in another place, “the Lord is nigh unto them who have bruised their heart.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who are they that have “bruised their heart”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who are they that have not “bruised their heart”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The bruised heart shall be healed, the puffed up heart shall be dashed down.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For for this purpose perhaps is it dashed down, that being bruised it may be healed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let not our heart then, brethren, desire to be set upright, before it be upright.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is ill for that to be uplifted which is not first corrected.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What are the means whereby He “bindeth up their bruises”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Just as physicians bind up fractures.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For sometimes (observe this, beloved; it is well known to those who have observed it, or have heard it from physicians), sometimes when limbs are sound, but are crooked and distorted, physicians break them in order to set them straight, and make a new wound, because the soundness which was distorted was amiss.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What are these means whereby He bindeth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The sacraments of this present life, whereby in the mean time we obtain our comfort: and all the words we speak to you, words which sound and pass away, all that is done in the Church in this present time, are the means whereby “He bindeth up our bruises.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For just as, when the limb has become perfectly sound, the physician taketh off the bandage; so in our own city Jerusalem, when we shall have been made equal to the Angels, think ye that we shall receive there, what we have received here?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Will it be needful then that the Gospel be read to us, that our faith may abide? or that hands be laid upon us by any Bishop?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All these are means of binding up fractures; when we have attained perfect soundness, they will be taken off; but we should never attain it, if they were not bound up.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who telleth the number of the stars, and calleth them all by their names” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What great matter is it for God to “tell the number of the stars”!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Men even have endeavoured to do this; whether they have been able to achieve it, is their concern; they would not however attempt it, did they not think that they should achieve it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us leave alone what they can do, and how far they have attained; for God I think it no great matter to count all the stars.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Or doth He perhaps go over the number, lest He should forget it?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is it any great thing for God to number the stars, by whom “the very hairs of your head are numbered”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The stars are certain lights in the Church comforting our night; all of whom the Apostle saith, “In the midst of a crooked and perverse generation, among whom ye shine as lights in the world, holding the Word of life.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These stars God counteth; all who shall reign with Him, all who are to be gathered into the Body of His only-begotten Son, He hath counted, and still counteth them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whoso is unworthy, is not even counted.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Many too have believed, or rather may, with a kind of shadowy appearance of faith, have attached themselves to His people: yet He knoweth what He counteth, what He winnoweth away.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For so great is the height of the Gospel, that it hath come to pass as was said, “I have declared, and have spoken: they are multiplied above number:” there are then among the people certain supernumeraries, so to speak.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What do I mean by supernumeraries?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: More than will be there.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Within these walls are more than will be in the kingdom of God, in the heavenly Jerusalem; these are above the number.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let each one of you consider whether he shineth in darkness, whether he refuseth to be led astray by the dark iniquity of the world; if he be not led astray, nor conquered, he will be, as it were, a star, which God already numbereth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And calling them all by their names,” he saith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Herein is our whole reward.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We may have certain names with God, that God may know our names, this we ought to wish, for this to act, for this to busy ourselves, as far as we are able; not to rejoice in other things, not even in certain spiritual gifts.…When the disciples returned from their mission exulting, and saying, “Lord, even the devils are subject unto us in Thy Name” —then He (knowing that many would say, “have we not in Thy Name cast out devils?” to whom He should say, “I know you not”) said, “In this rejoice not, that the devils are subject unto you; but rather rejoice, because your names are written in heaven.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Great is our Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Psalmist is filled with joy, he hath poured out his words wonderfully: yet somewhat he was unable to speak, and how availed he to think on it?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And great is His power, and of His understanding is no numbering.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who “numbereth the stars,” Himself cannot be numbered.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who can expound this? who can worthily even imagine what is meant by, “and of His understanding is no number”?…Whatsoever then that is infinite this world containeth, though it be infinite to man, yet is not to God: too little is it to say, to God: even by the angels it is numbered.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: His understanding surpasses all calculators; it cannot be counted by us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Numbers themselves who numbereth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What than is there with God? wherewith made He all things, and where made He all things, to whom it is said, “Thou hast arrayed all things in measure, number, and weight”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Or who can number, or measure, or weigh, measure and number and weight themselves, wherein God hath ordered all things?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore, “of His understanding is no number.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let human voices be hushed, human thoughts still: let them not stretch themselves out to incomprehensible things, as though they could comprehend them, but as though they were to partake of them, for partakers we shall be.…Partakers then we shall be: let none doubt it: Scripture saith it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And of what shall we be partakers, as though these were parts in God, as though God were divided into parts?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who then can explain how many become partakers of one single substance?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Require not then that which I think ye see cannot fitly be said: but return to the healing of the Saviour, bruise your heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He will guide it, He will bind it up where it is broken, He will make it perfectly sound; and then those things will not be impossible with us, which now are impossible.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is good that he confess weakness, who desireth to attain to the divine nature.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord taketh up the gentle” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For example; thou understandest not, thou failest to understand, canst not attain: honour God’s Scripture, honour God’s Word, though it be not plain: in reverence wait for understanding.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be not wanton to accuse either the obscurity or seeming contradiction of Scripture.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is nothing in it contradictory: somewhat there is which is obscure, not in order that it may be denied thee, but that it may exercise him that shall afterward receive it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When then it is obscure, that is the Physician’s doing, that thou mayest knock.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He willed that thou shouldest be exercised in knocking; He willed it, that He might open to thee when thou knockest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By knocking thou shalt be exercised; exercised, thou shalt be enlarged; enlarged, thou shalt contain what is given.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be not then indignant for that it is shut; be mild, be gentle.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Kick not against what is dark, nor say, It were better said, if it were said thus.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For how canst thou thus say, or judge how it is expedient it be said?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is said as it is expedient it be said.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let not the sick man seek to amend his remedies: the Physician knoweth how to temper them: believe Him who careth for thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore what cometh next?…“The Lord taketh up the gentle, but humbleth the sinners even to the ground,” he intended a certain sort of sinners to be understood, from the gentleness mentioned first.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By sinners then in this place, we understand the fierce, and those who are not gentle.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore doth He “humble them even to the earth”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They carp at objects of understanding, they shall perceive only things earthly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Begin to the Lord in confession” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Begin with this, if thou wouldest arrive at a clear understanding of the truth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou wilt be brought from the road of faith to the profession of the reality, “begin in confession.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: First accuse thyself: accuse thyself, praise God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What after confession?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let good works follow.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Sing unto our God upon the harp.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “Upon the harp”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As I have already explained, just like the Psalm upon the psaltery, so also is the “harp:” not with voice only, but with works.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 12. …“Who covereth the heaven with clouds, who prepareth rain for the earth” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now thou art alarmed, because thou canst not see the heaven: when it hath rained thou shalt gather fruit, and shalt see clear sky.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Perhaps our God hath done this.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For had we not the obscurity of Scripture as an occasion, we should not say to you those things wherein ye rejoice.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This then perhaps is the rain whereat ye rejoice.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It would not be possible for it to be expressed to you by our tongue, were it not that God covereth with clouds of figures the heaven of the Scriptures.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this purpose willed He that the words of the Prophets should be obscure, that the servants of God might afterwards have that by interpreting which they might flow over the ears and hearts of men, that they might receive from the clouds of God the fatness of spiritual joy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who maketh grass to grow upon the mountains, and herb for the service of men.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold the fruit of the rain.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who maketh,” saith he, “grass to grow upon the mountains.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Doth it not also grow upon the low ground?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yes, but it is a great thing that it groweth “on the mountains.”…For nothing could be more barren than the hard mountains.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And herb for the service of men.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What “service”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Listen to Paul himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And ourselves,” saith he, “your servants for Jesus Christ’s sake.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who said, “If we have sown unto you spiritual things, is it a great thing if we reap your carnal things?” yet said, that he was a “servant.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we are your servants, brethren.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let none of us speak of himself, as though he were greater than you.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We shall be greater if we are more humble.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But whosoever will be great among you” (it is the Lord’s saying), “shall be your servant.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Paul the Apostle, indeed, living by his own labour, refused even to receive “the grass of the mountains;” he chose to want; nevertheless, the mountains gave “grass.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because he chose not to receive, ought the mountains therefore not to give, and so to remain barren?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Fruit is due to the rain, food is due to the servant, as the Lord saith, “Eat such things as they give you:” and that they should not think that they gave aught of their own, He added, “for the labourer is worthy of his hire.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 13. …Just now has been read, “Give to every one that asketh of thee;” and in another place Scripture saith, “Let alms sweat in thy hand, till thou findest a righteous man to whom to give it.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: One there is who seeketh thee, another thou oughtest to seek.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Leave not indeed him who seeketh thee empty, for, “give to every one that asketh of thee;” yet still there is another whom thou oughtest to seek; “find a righteous man to whom to give it.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye will never do this, unless ye have somewhat set aside from your substance, each what pleaseth him according to the needs of his family, as a sort of debt to be paid to the treasury.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If Christ have not a state of His own, neither hath He a treasury.…Cut off then and prune off some fixed sum either from thy yearly profits or thy daily gains, else thou seemest as it were to give of thy capital, and thy hand must needs hesitate, when thou puttest it forth to that which thou hast not vowed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Cut off some part of thy income; a tenth if thou choosest, though that is but little.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is said that the Pharisees gave a tenth; “I fast twice in the week, I give tithes of all that I possess.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what saith the Lord?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Except your righteousness exceed the righteousness of the Scribes and Pharisees, ye shall not enter into the kingdom of heaven.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He whose righteousness thou oughtest to exceed, giveth a tenth: thou givest not even a thousandth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How wilt thou surpass him whom thou matchest not?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who prepareth rain for the earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And giveth unto the cattle their food” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These are the cattle he meaneth, even God’s flocks.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God defraudeth not His flock of their food through men, for whose “service He maketh the grass to grow.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And to the young of the ravens that call upon Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Shall we perchance think this, that the ravens call upon God to give them their food?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Think not that the unreasoning creature calls upon God: no creature knows how to call upon God, save the reasonable alone.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Consider it as spoken in a figure, lest thou think, as some evil men say, that the souls of men migrate into cattle, dogs, swine, ravens.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Give this no place in your hearts or in your faith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The soul of man is made after the image of God: He will not give His image to dog or swine.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who are “the young of the ravens”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Israelites used to say that they alone were righteous, because to them the Law had been given: all other men of every nation they used to call sinners.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And in truth all nations were given up to sin, to idolatry, to the worship of stones and stocks: but did they continue so?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Although the ravens themselves, our fathers, did not, yet we, “the young of the ravens,” do call upon God.…For “the young of the ravens,” who seemed to worship the images of their forefathers, have advanced, and turned to God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And now thou hearest “the young of the ravens” calling upon the one God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Sayest thou to “the young of the ravens,” “hast thou left thy father?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Plainly I have, saith he; for he is a raven who calls not upon God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I, “the young of the raven,” do call upon God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In the power of an horse He will not take pleasure” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The power “of an horse” is pride.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the horse seemeth adapted as it were to bear a man aloft, that he may be more uplifted as he goes.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And in truth he has a neck which typifieth a sort of pride.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let not men exalt themselves upon their worth, let them not think themselves uplifted by their distinctions; let them beware lest they be thrown by an untamed horse.…“Nor in the tabernacle of a man will He delight.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the tabernacle of the Lord is the Holy Church spread throughout the whole world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Heretics, separating themselves from the Church’s tabernacles, have set up tabernacles for themselves.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if perchance it be the lot of any, who is good and pious, who confesseth his own weakness, who is “the young of a raven that calleth on God,” not to enjoy worldly distinction, he goeth not out of the Church, he setteth not up for himself a tent outside the Church, wherein God will not delight.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what saith he?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have chosen to be cast away in the house of God, rather than to dwell in the tents of sinners.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what addeth he?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord will delight in them that fear Him, and in them that hope in His mercy” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A robber is feared, and a wild beast is feared, and an unjust and powerful man is much feared.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord will delight in them that hope in His mercy.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold, Judas, who betrayed our Lord, feared, but he did not hope in His mercy.…It is well indeed that thou hast feared, but only if thou trustedst in His mercy, whom thou hast feared.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He in despair “went and hanged himself.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In such wise then fear the Lord, that thou trust in His mercy.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Praise in unison, O Jerusalem, thy God” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Abiding yet in captivity, they behold those flocks, or rather, the one flock of all its citizens, gathered from all sides into that city; they see the joy of the mass, now after threshings and winnowings placed in the garner, fearing nothing, suffering no toil nor trouble; and, as yet abiding here, in the midst of the threshing they send forward their joy of hope, and pant for it, joining as it were their hearts to the Angels of God, and to that people which shall abide with them in joy for ever.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what wilt thou then do, O Jerusalem?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Surely toil and groaning will pass away.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What wilt thou do? wilt thou plough, or sow, or plant vines, or make voyages, or trade?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What wilt thou do?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Will it still be thy duty to be engaged in the works thou now doest, good though they are, and spring from mercy?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Consider thy numbers, consider on all sides thy company: see whether any hungers, for thee to give bread to; see whether any thirsts, for thee to give a cup of cold water to; see whether any is a stranger, for thee to take in; see whether any is sick, for thee to visit; see whether any is at strife, for thee to reconcile him; see whether any is dying, for thee to bury him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then wilt thou do?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Praise in unison, O Jerusalem, thy God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold, this is thy business.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As is wont to be said in inscriptions, “Use it and be happy.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be ye Jerusalem; remember of whom it is said, “Lord, in Thy city their image Thou shalt bring to nought.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These are they who now rejoice in such pomps; among them are they who have not come hither to-day because there is a show.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To whom is it a gift? to whom is it a loss? or why is it a gift? why is it a loss?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For not they only who exhibit such shows are smitten with loss, but with much greater loss are they smitten who delight in gazing on them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The former have their chest drained of its gold, the latter have their breast robbed of the riches of righteousness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Most of the exhibiters of shows have to mourn for selling their estates; how ought the sinners to mourn, for losing their souls: Was it then for this that the Lord cried out on the Lord’s Day, “Watch ye,” that to-day men should watch in this way.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I beseech you, ye citizens of Jerusalem, I beseech you by the peace of Jerusalem, by the Redeemer, the Builder, the Ruler of Jerusalem, that ye address your prayers to God for them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: May they see, may they feel, that they are trifling; and, intent as they are on the sights which please them, may at length look on themselves, and be displeased.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in many we rejoice that this has already been done: and once we too sat there and were mad: and how many think we now sit there, who shall yet be, not only Christians, but also Bishops!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From what is past, we conjecture what is to be: from what has already been done, we announce beforehand what God will do.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let your prayers be wakeful, ye groan not for nothing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Certainly they who have already escaped, praying for those who are still in danger, because they too having been among those in danger, are heard; and God shall drag His people out of the captivity of Babylon; by all means He shall redeem and deliver them, and the number of the saints who bear the image of God shall be perfected.…“Praise in unison,” because thou consistest of many: “praise,” because thou hast been made one.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “We being many,” saith the Apostle, “are one in Christ.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As then we are many, “we praise in unison;” as we are one, we “praise.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The same are many and one, because He in whom they are one is ever One.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore, saith this Jerusalem, do I praise in unison the Lord, and, as Sion, praise my God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Jerusalem is the same as Sion.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For different reasons has it the two names.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Jerusalem meaneth “visions of peace;” Sion meaneth “watching.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See whether these words do not sound like sights; that the Gentiles may not think that they have sights and we have none.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Sometimes after the theatre or amphitheatre breaks up, when the crowd of lost ones begins to be vomited forth from that den, sometimes, retaining in their minds images of their vain amusements, and feeding their memory with things not only useless but even hurtful, rejoicing in them as if they were sweet, while they are really deadly; they see often, it may be, the servants of God pass by, they recognise them by their garb or headdress, or they know them by sight, and they say to one another, or inwardly, “Wretched people, how much they lose!”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Brethren, let us return their good will (for they do mean it well) with prayers to the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They wish us well; but “he that loveth iniquity, hateth his own soul.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If he hateth his own soul, how shall he love my soul?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet with a perverse, and empty, and vain good will, if indeed it may be called good will, they grieve that we lose what they love: let us pray that they lose not what we love.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold of what character that Jerusalem is to be which he exhorteth to praise, or rather foreseeth will praise.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the praises of that city, when we shall see and love and praise, will not need to be urged on and stirred up by the voice of prophecy; but the Prophets now say this, to drink in as far as while they remain in this flesh they can, the future joys of the blessed, and then giving them forth into our ears, to arouse in us love of that city.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us burn with longing, let us not be slothful in spirit.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Praise thy God, O Sion.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He saith, “He hath made strong the bars of thy gates” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The making bars strong is not for open gates, but shut ones, wherefore most manuscripts read, “He hath made strong the bolts of thy gates.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Observe, beloved.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He biddeth Jerusalem when closed in to praise the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We praise in unison now, we praise now; but it is amid offences.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Many where we wish not, enter in: many though we wish it not, go out: therefore offences are frequent.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And because iniquity hath abounded,” saith the Truth, “the love of many waxeth cold:” because men come in whom we cannot discern, because men go out whom we cannot retain.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore is this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because not yet is there perfection, not yet is there the bliss that shall be.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore is this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because as yet it is the threshing-floor, not yet the garner.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What therefore will be then, save no fear that aught of this kind will happen?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He said not only, He hath set, but, “He hath made strong the bars of thy gates.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let none go out, let none come in.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let none go out, we rejoice: let none come in, we fear.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nay, fear not this: when thou hast entered it will be said: only be thou in the number of virgins, who carried their oil with them.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He hath blessed thy children within thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He “who hath set peace as thy borders.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How ye all exult!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Love peace, my brethren.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Greatly are we delighted, when the love of peace crieth from your hearts.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How greatly doth it delight you!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I had said nothing: I had explained nothing: I but read the verse, and ye shouted.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What was it that shouted in you?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The love of peace.…children of the kingdom, O citizens of Jerusalem, in Jerusalem is the vision of peace: and all who love peace are blest in her, and they enter in, when the doors are being shut, and the bars made strong.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This, which when but named ye so love and esteem, this follow after, this long for: this love in your home, in your business, in your wives, in your sons, in your slaves, in your friends, in your enemies.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What ye cried out a while ago at the very mention of peace, ye cried from longing: your cry was from thirst, not from fulness; for there will be perfect righteousness where will be perfect peace.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now we hunger and thirst after righteousness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They shall be filled.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How shall they be filled?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When we have arrived at peace.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore when he had said, “Who hath set peace for thy borders,” because there is fulness and no want, he added at once, “and filleth thee with the fat of wheat” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who sendeth forth His Word to the earth” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold, on earth we toil, weary, fainting, sluggish, cold: when should we be raised up to the fat of wheat that satisfieth, did not He send His Word to the earth, whereby we were weighed down, to the earth, whereby we were hindered from returning?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He sent His Word, He deserted us not even in the wilderness, He rained manna from heaven.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who sendeth forth His Word to the earth;” and to earth His Word came.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How? or what is His Word?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Even unto swiftness His Word runneth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He said not, “His Word is swift,” but, “His Word runneth even unto swiftness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us understand, my brethren: He could not have chosen a better word.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He who is hot grows hot by heat, he who is cold grows cold by cold, he who is swift becometh swift by swiftness.…To what degree then doth it run?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Even to swiftness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Increase as much as you will the swiftness of the Word, and say, It is as swift as this or that, as birds, as the winds, as the Angels; is any of these as great as swiftness itself, “even unto swiftness”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is swiftness itself, brethren?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is everywhere; it is not in part.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This belongeth to the Word of God, not to be in part, to be everywhere by Himself the Word, whereby He is “the Power of God and the Wisdom of God,” before He had taken flesh upon Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If we think of God in the Form of God, the Word equal to the Father, this is the Wisdom of God, of which is said, “It reacheth from one end to the other mightily.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What mighty speed!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “It reacheth from one end to the other mightily.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We then are burdened by the sluggishness of this cold body, and the bonds of this earthly and corruptible life; have we no hope of receiving “the Word,” which “runneth even unto swiftness”? or hath abandoned us, though by the body we are depressed to the lowest depths?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Did not He predestinate us, before we were born in this mortal and sluggish body?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He then, who predestinated us, gave snow to the earth, even ourselves.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For now let us come to those somewhat obscure verses of the Psalm, let those entanglements begin to be unrolled.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold, we are sluggish on this earth, and are as it were frozen here.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And just as happens to the flakes of snow, for they freeze above, then fall down; so as love groweth cold, human nature falleth down to this earth, and involved in a sluggish body becometh like snow.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But in that snow are predestined sons of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For, “He giveth snow like wool” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is “like wool”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It meaneth, of the snow which He hath given, of these, who are as yet slow in spirit and cold, whom He hath predestinated, He is about to make somewhat.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For wool is the material of a garment: when we see wool, we look on it as a sort of preparation for a garment.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore since He hath predestinated these, who at present are cold and creep on earth, and as yet glow not with the spirit of love (for as yet He speaketh of predestination), God hath given these as a sort of wool: He is about to make of them a garment.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Rightly did the “raiment” of Christ “shine” on the mountain, “like snow.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The raiment of Christ did shine like snow, as though of that snow a garment had already been made: of which wool, that is, of the snow which He gave like wool, they being as yet predestined, were sluggish: but wait, see what followeth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Since He gave them as wool, a garment is made of them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For as the Church is called the Body of Christ, so is the Church also called the garment of Christ: hence cometh that which is said by the Apostle, “that He might present it to Himself a glorious Church, not having spot or wrinkle.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let Him then present unto Himself a glorious Church, not having spot or wrinkle; let Him make Himself a garment of that wool, which He had predestinated in the snow.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: While men are yet unbelieving, and cold, and sluggish, let Him make a garment of this wool.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That it may be washed from spots, let it be cleansed by faith: that it may have no wrinkle, let it be stretched out upon the cross.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He scattereth mist like ashes.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He scattereth,” saith the Psalmist, “mist like ashes.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He “who giveth snow like wool.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For whom He predestined, He calleth to repentance; for “whom He predestined, them He also called.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But “ashes” are connected with repentance.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear Him calling to repentance, when He upbraided certain cities, saying, “Woe unto thee, Chorazin! woe unto thee, Bethsaida! for if the mighty works which have been done in you had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they had long ago repented in dust and ashes.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore, “He scattereth mist like ashes.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “He scattereth mist like ashes”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When a man is called to learn about God, and it is said to him, “Receive the truth;” he beginneth to wish to receive the Truth, but is not able; he seeth that He is under a sort of darkness, which before he saw not.…Wander not in the mist, follow in faith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But forasmuch as thou endeavourest to see and art not able, repent of thy sins, for mist is scattered like ashes.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Repent thee now of having been obstinate against God, repent of having followed thine own evil ways.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast come into this state where it is difficult for thee to see the vision of bliss, and the mist will be healthful to thee, which God scattereth like ashes.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou thyself art as yet a mist, but like ashes.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they that are penitent, as yet roll themselves in ashes, my brethren, testifying, as it were, that they are like it, saying unto God, “I am ashes.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For a certain Scripture saith, “I have despised myself, and wasted away, I have reckoned myself earth and ashes.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the humility of the penitent.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When Abraham speaketh to his God, and wisheth the burning of Sodom to be disclosed to him, he saith, “I am but earth and ashes.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How hath this humility ever been found in great and holy men!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who sendeth His crystal like morsels of bread” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We need not spend our toil again in saying what crystal is.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We have already said it, and I do not think that ye, beloved, have forgotten it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is then, “He sendeth His crystal like morsels of bread”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is “crystal”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is very hard, it is very tightly congealed; it can not, like snow, be easily melted.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Snow, hardened by many years’ duration, and by the succession of ages, is called “crystal,” and this “He sendeth like morsels of bread.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What meaneth this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They were too hard, no longer fit to be compared to snow, but to crystal; but they too are predestinated and called, and some of them even so as to feed others, to be useful to others also.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what need is there to enumerate many, whom we happen to know, this one and that one?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Every one when he thinks can recall to mind how hardened and obstinate some of those whom he knows have been, how they have struggled against the truth; yet now they preach the truth, they have been made morsels of bread.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who is that one Bread?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “We being many,” saith the Apostle, “are one Body in Christ;” he saith also, “we being many are one Bread and one Body.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If then the whole Body of Christ is one Bread, the members of Christ are morsels of Bread.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of some that are hard He maketh members of Himself, and useful for feeding others.…Behold, the Apostle Paul was a crystal, hard, resisting the truth, crying out against the Gospel, hardening himself, as it were, against the sun.…Since then he was crystal, he appeared clear and white, but he was hard and very cold.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How was he bright and white?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “An Hebrew of the Hebrews; as touching the law, a Pharisee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold the brightness of crystal.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now hear the hardness of crystal.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “As touching zeal, persecuting the Church” of Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Among the stoners of the holy martyr Stephen, was he, hard, perhaps harder than all.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For he kept the raiment of all who were stoning,” so that he stoned by the hands of all.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus then we see “the snow, the mist, the crystal:” it is good that He blow and thaw them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if He blow not, if He Himself thaw not the hardness of this ice, “in the face of His cold who shall stand?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He abandoneth a sinner, behold, He calleth him not; behold, He openeth not his perception; behold, He poureth not in grace; let the man thaw himself, if he can, from the ice of folly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore can he not?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In the face of His cold who shall stand?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold him then growing harder, and saying, “O wretched man that I am! who shall deliver me from the body of this death?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold, I am growing cold, behold, I am growing hard, what heat shall thaw me that I may run?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who shall deliver me from the body of this death?.…In the face of His cold who shall stand?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And who shall free himself, if God abandon him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who is it that freeth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The grace of God, through Jesus Christ our Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Are we then to despair?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it goeth on, “He shall send out His Word, and melt them” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let not then the snow despair, nor the mist, nor the crystal.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For of the snow, as of wool, a garment is being made.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That mist findeth safety in repentance: for, “whom He predestinated, them He also called.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But even though they be the very hardest among the predestinated, though they have been for a long time hardening, and are become crystal, they will not be hard to the mercy of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He shall send out His Word, and melt them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is “melt”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Understand not “melt” in an ill sense: it meaneth, He shall liquefy, He shall thaw them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they are hard through pride.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Rightly is pride called also dulness: for whatever is dull, is also cold.…Despair not even of the crystal.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear a saying of the crystal.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who before was a blasphemer, and a persecutor, and injurious.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But wherefore doth God melt the crystal?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That the snow despair not of itself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he saith, “For this cause I obtained mercy, that in me first Jesus Christ might show forth all long-suffering, for a pattern to them that hereafter should believe on Him unto eternal life.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God then calleth unto the Gentiles, “Be melted, O crystal; come, ye snows.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “His Spirit shall blow, and the waters shall flow.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lo, the “crystal” and the “snows” are melted, they turn into water, “let them that thirst, come and drink.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Saul, hard as crystal, persecuted Stephen unto death; Paul, now in the living water, calleth the Gentiles to the Fount.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Announcing His Word unto Jacob, His Righteousnesses and Judgments unto Israel” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What “Righteousnesses,” what “Judgments”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because whatever mankind had suffered here before, when it was “snow” and “mist” and “crystal,” it suffered for the deserts of its pride and uplifting against God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us go back to the origin of our fall, and see that most truly is it sung in the Psalm, “Before I was troubled I went wrong.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But he who says, “Before I was troubled I went wrong,” saith also, “It is good for me that Thou hast humbled me, that I may learn Thy Righteousnesses.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These righteousnesses Jacob learnt from God, who made him to wrestle with an Angel; under the guise of which Angel, God Himself wrestled with him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He held Him, he exerted violence to hold Him, he prevailed to hold Him: He caused Himself to be held, in mercy, not in weakness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Jacob therefore wrestled, and prevailed: he held Him and when he seemed to have conquered Him asked to be blessed of Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How did he understand with Whom he had wrestled, Whom he had held?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore did he wrestle violently, and hold Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because “the kingdom of heaven suffereth violence, and the violent take it by force.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore then did he wrestle?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because it is with toil.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore do we with difficulty hold, what we so easily lose?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lest, easily getting back what we have lost, we learn to lose that which we hold.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let man have toil to hold: he will hold firmly, what he has only held after toil.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These His judgments therefore God manifested to Jacob and Israel.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He hath not done so to the whole race” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let none deceive you: it is not announced to any nation, this judgment of God; namely, how the righteous and the unrighteous suffer, how all suffer for their deserts, how the righteous themselves are freed by the grace of God, not in their own merits.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is not announced to the whole race, but only to Jacob, only to Israel.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then do we, if He hath not announced it to the whole race, but only to Jacob, only to Israel?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where will we be?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He hath not manifested His judgments to them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To all nations.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How then are the “snows” called, when the crystal is melted?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How are the nations called, now Paul is justified?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How, save to be in Jacob?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The wild olive is cut off from its stock, to be grafted into the olive: now they belong to the olive, no longer ought they to be called nations, but one nation in Christ, the nation of Jacob, the nation of Israel…What is Israel?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Seeing God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where shall he see God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The peace of Jerusalem; for, saith he, “He hath set peace for thy borders.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There shall we praise: there shall we all be one, in One, unto One: for then, though many, we shall not be scattered.
